name: Volumetric (Dimensional) Weight, metric divisor
description: Volumetric (dimensional/DIM) weight in kilograms: total shipment volume in cubic centimetres divided by a carrier-published divisor in cm3 per kg (e.g. 6000 for general air cargo, 5000 for express couriers). Carriers bill light-but-bulky freight on this figure instead of the scale weight.

inputs.name: Cartons
inputs.description: Number of identical cartons in the shipment.

inputs.name: Divisor
inputs.description: Carrier-published DIM divisor; must be greater than zero. Common metric values: 6000 (IATA air convention), 5000 (DHL Express and other couriers).

outputs.name: Volumetric weight
outputs.description: Dimensional weight the carrier compares against actual weight when rating.

assumptions: Dimensions are entered in centimetres and the divisor is in cm3 per kg, so the result is kilograms.
All cartons share the same outer dimensions.
No carrier rounding is applied inside the formula; carriers typically round dimensions and the final billable weight by their own rules.
limitations: Divisors are carrier-published and vary by carrier, service, rate card, country, account agreement, and date — e.g. DHL Express publishes 5000 cm3/kg, the IATA air convention is 6000 cm3/kg, and US parcel carriers use imperial divisors (FedEx 139 in3/lb; UPS 139 Daily Rates / 166 Retail Rates; USPS 166 in3/lb until 2026-07-11, 139 from 2026-07-12). Treat any preset divisor as an estimate and confirm against the carrier's current service guide.
The imperial variant of this formula uses inches and a divisor in in3/lb; do not mix unit systems within one calculation.
Carrier rounding rules differ (nearest vs round-up inch, round up to next lb or 0.5 kg) and can move the billed figure above this raw result.
Some carriers apply dimensional weight only above a size threshold (e.g. USPS applies DIM pricing above 1,728 in3).

examples.note: Ten cartons of 120 x 80 x 100 cm at the 6000 air divisor: 9,600,000 cm3 / 6000 = 1,600 kg.

sources: DHL Express volumetric weight calculator page (dhlexpress.ee/en/terms-and-conditions-2/volumetric-weight-calculator/, checked 2026-07-02): L x W x H in cm / 5000 for kg, and / 139 for in/lb.
FedEx 'What is dimensional weight?' page (fedex.com/en-us/shipping/packaging/what-is-dimensional-weight.html, checked 2026-07-02): L x W x H in inches / 139 for US/Puerto Rico/international packages.

UPS 'Shipping Dimensions and Weight' support page (ups.com/us/en/support/shipping-support/shipping-dimensions-weight, checked 2026-07-02): divisor 139 for Daily Rates, 166 for Retail Rates; DIM result rounded up to the next whole pound.
IATA TACT air-cargo convention: volumetric weight at 6000 cm3/kg (equivalently 1:6000 or 167 kg/m3) for general air freight.

USPS Postal Explorer QSG 150 (pe.usps.com/text/qsg300/q150.htm) shows divisor 166 for qualifying parcels as of 2026-07-02; the Federal Register final rule of 2026-05-15 changes it to 139 effective 2026-07-12.
